rationale: Recent active-side centralized exchange incident batch covering CoinDCX and Bybit. CoinDCX is modeled as a new active CEX record with July 2025 internal operational-account hack and withdrawal/service-restoration events. Bybit is already canonical as hei_ex_000011; this package adds February 2025 hack and recovery/reserve-gap events only, preserving Bybit's active global status.

entities.summary: Indian centralized cryptocurrency exchange that confirmed a July 2025 security breach involving an internal operational account, with reports placing the loss at about USD 44 million. CoinDCX said customer funds were not affected and later restored normal withdrawal/service operations.
entities.notes: Do not mark dead. The July 2025 incident involved an internal operational account according to company statements and reporting, not customer-custody loss. Track hack/security incident and restoration events while keeping status active.

events.title: CoinDCX confirmed an internal operational-account hack
events.description: CoinDCX confirmed a security breach involving an internal operational account, with reports estimating about USD 44 million stolen; the company said customer funds were not affected.
events.notes: Use careful wording: sources report that an internal operational account was affected, while customer funds were said to be safe.

events.title: CoinDCX paused or limited some services after the breach
events.description: Reports described temporary product/service restrictions after the breach, including Web3 / INR and crypto-withdrawal handling, while CoinDCX worked to contain the incident and reassure users about fund safety.
events.notes: Modeled as withdrawal_suspended because the service impact centered on transfer/withdrawal handling rather than a permanent exchange halt.

events.title: CoinDCX resumed withdrawals after the breach
events.description: Indian business press reported that CoinDCX resumed withdrawals after the USD 44 million breach and reiterated that user funds were safe.

events.title: Bybit suffered a record-scale Ethereum wallet hack
events.description: Bybit suffered a record-scale Ethereum wallet hack in February 2025, with reports estimating about USD 1.4-1.5 billion stolen from a cold-wallet transfer/signing process while the exchange continued operating globally.
events.notes: Existing Bybit entity remains active. This event should not be interpreted as a global shutdown.

events.title: Bybit closed its ETH reserve gap after the hack
events.description: Bybit said or was reported to have closed the ETH gap after the hack by acquiring or securing large amounts of ETH, restoring 1:1 backing and continuing operations.
events.notes: Recovery/reserve-gap event supports active classification despite the critical hack.

evidence.title: Indian crypto exchange CoinDCX confirms $44 million stolen during hack
evidence.notes: Used for CoinDCX hack confirmation, approximately USD 44M amount, internal operational account wording, and user-funds-safe claim.

evidence.title: Indian Crypto Exchange CoinDCX Suffers $44M Hack
evidence.notes: Primary market-news source for the approximately USD 44M incident and initial exchange response.

evidence.title: CoinDCX resumes withdrawals after $44 million security breach, assures user funds are safe
evidence.notes: Used for withdrawal/service restoration and repeated user-funds-safe statement.

evidence.title: Crypto exchange Bybit says $1.5bn worth of digital coins stolen by hackers
evidence.notes: Used for the approximate USD 1.5B amount, hack framing, and stolen-ETH context.

evidence.title: Bybit Closes ETH Gap as Exchange Amasses $1.2B After Hack
evidence.notes: Used for ETH-gap closure, post-hack recapitalization, and continuing operations.

evidence.title: Crypto platform Bybit closes $1.5 billion gap following largest crypto heist of all time
evidence.notes: Supporting source for large-heist framing and reserve-gap closure.

manual_review_checklist: Confirm CoinDCX is not already canonical before apply; if it exists, update rather than duplicate.
Use existing Bybit entity hei_ex_000011 for Bybit hack/recovery events.
Keep CoinDCX and Bybit active; neither event is a shutdown record.
Use event ids hei_ev_000397 through hei_ev_000401 only if available.
Use evidence ids hei_src_000805 through hei_src_000810 only if available.
